title: Apparatus and method for ue in wireless communication system
abstract: A method of operating a UE in a wireless communication system includes communicating traffic, over a time step, based on a first set of RF parameters received from a network device, classifying the traffic into a traffic class, selecting a second set of RF parameters based on the traffic class, estimating a throughput demand and a throughput supply, and determining, based on the estimated throughput demand and the estimated throughput supply, whether an anomalous traffic condition has occurred, if the anomalous traffic condition has not occurred, transmitting a first request to the network device to configure the UE with the second set of RF parameters, and if an anomalous traffic condition has occurred, transmitting a second request to the network device to configure the UE with a third set of RF parameters selected to alleviate the anomalous traffic condition.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A user equipment, UE, (116) in a wireless communication system, the UE comprising: memory (360) storing instructions; at least one processor (340) configured to, when executing the instructions, cause the UE to perform operations comprising: communicating (2010) traffic, over a time step, based on a first set of radio frequency, RF, parameters received from a network device (102), classifying (2020) the traffic into a traffic class, selecting (2030) a second set of RF parameters based on the traffic class, estimating (2040) a throughput demand and a throughput supply;, determining (2050), based on the estimated throughput demand and the estimated throughput supply, whether an anomalous traffic condition has occurred, if the anomalous traffic condition has not occurred, transmitting (2060) a first request to the network device to configure the UE with the second set of RF parameters, and if the anomalous traffic condition has occurred, transmitting (2070) a second request to the network device to configure the UE with a third set of RF parameters selected to alleviate the anomalous traffic condition. The UE of claim 1, wherein determining whether the anomalous traffic condition has occurred comprises determining that the anomalous traffic condition has not occurred based on the estimated throughput supply exceeding the estimated throughput demand by a threshold, and wherein the first request to the network device to configure the UE with the second set of RF parameters is transmitted based on the first set of RF parameters being not identical to the second set of RF parameters, and a UE assistance information, UAI, timer being expired. The UE of claim 1, wherein determining whether the anomalous traffic condition has occurred comprises determining that the anomalous traffic condition has occurred based on the estimated throughput supply not exceeding the estimated throughput demand by a threshold, wherein the second request to the network device to configure the UE with the third set of RF parameters is transmitted based on the first set of RF parameters being not identical to the third set of RF parameters and an anomaly timer being expired. The UE of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: determining a channel quality indicator, CQI; and estimating, based on the past observations of MCS for a given CQI, a modulation coding scheme, MCS, and wherein the throughput supply is estimated based on the estimated MCS. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises: determining a present throughput observation corresponding to the traffic; determining if a throughput observation database comprises more than one throughput observation; if the throughput observation database does not comprise more than one observation, updating the throughput observation database with the present throughput observation; and if the throughput observation database comprises more than one throughput observation: determining, based on a statistical analysis of the throughput observation database, a z score for the present throughput observation; updating, based on the z score, a value of a throughput observation counter; and updating the throughput observation database based on the updated value of the throughput observation counter, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the updated throughput observation database. The UE of claim 5, wherein the operations further comprise: if an absolute value of the updated value of the throughput observation counter is N, updating the throughput observation database to retain only N most recent throughput observations; and if an absolute value of the updated value of the throughput observation counter is less than N, updating the throughput observation database to include the present throughput observation; and if the throughput observation database comprises at least K observations, updating the throughput observation database to remove an oldest throughput observation, wherein N indicates how many throughput observations should deviate from a result of the statistical analysis before determining that a present throughput has substantially changed from a previous throughput, and wherein K is a large enough number to obtain a statistical analysis. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises: determining a mean and a scaled standard deviation of a plurality of throughput observations comprised by a throughput observation database, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the mean and the scaled standard deviation. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises determining a mean and a percentile of a plurality of throughput observations comprised by a throughput observation database, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the percentile. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises: determining a transport block size, TBS, for all transport blocks transmitted over the time step; normalizing a total of the TBS for the transport blocks transmitted over the time step based on a size of the time step; and normalizing a total of the TBS for the transport blocks received over the time step  based on the size of the time step, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the normalized total of the TBS for the transport blocks transmitted over the time step and the normalized total of the TBS for the transport blocks received over the time step. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises: determining a packet size for all packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID transmitted over the time step; determining a packet size for all packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID received over the time step; normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID transmitted over the time step based on a size of the time step; and normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID received over the time step based on the size of the time step, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the normalized total of the packet sizes for the packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID transmitted over the time step and the normalized total of the packet sizes for the packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID received over the time step. The UE of claim 1, wherein estimating the throughput demand comprises: segregating all packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID transmitted over the time step into real time, RT, uplink, UL, packets and non-real time, NRT, UL packets; segregating all packets comprising a five tuple including a process ID received over the time step into RT downlink, DL, packets and NRT DL packets; determining a packet size for all RT UL packets; determining a packet size for all NRT UL packets; determining a packet size for all RT DL packets; determining a packet size for all NRT DL packets; normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the RT UL packets based on a size of the time step; and normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the NRT UL packets based on the size of the time step; normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the RT DL packets based on the size of the time step; and normalizing a total of the packet sizes for the NRT DL packets based on the size of the time step, wherein the throughput demand is estimated based on the normalized totals of the packet sizes for the RT UL packets, the NRT UL packets, the RT DL packets, and the NRT DL packets. A method of operating a user equipment, UE, (116) in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: communicating (2010) traffic, over a time step, based on a first set of radio frequency, RF, parameters received from a network device (102); classifying (2020) the traffic into a traffic class; selecting (2030) a second set of RF parameters based on the traffic class; estimating (2040) a throughput demand and a throughput supply; determining (2050), based on the estimated throughput demand and the estimated throughput supply, whether an anomalous traffic condition has occurred; if the anomalous traffic condition has not occurred, transmitting (2060) a first request to the network device to configure the UE with the second set of RF parameters; and if the anomalous traffic condition has occurred, transmitting (2070) a second request to the network device to configure the UE with a third set of RF parameters selected to alleviate the anomalous traffic condition. The method of claim 12, further comprising at least one operation performed by the UE of one of claims 2 to 11. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing instructions which, when executed by at least one processor (340) of a user equipment, UE, (116) cause the UE perform operations, the operations comprising: communicating (2010) traffic, over a time step, based on a first set of radio frequency (RF) parameters received from a network device (102); classifying (2020) the traffic into a traffic class; selecting (2030) a second set of RF parameters based on the traffic class; estimating (2040) a throughput demand and a throughput supply; determining (2050), based on the estimated throughput demand and the estimated throughput supply, whether an anomalous traffic condition has occurred; if the anomalous traffic condition has not occurred, transmitting (2060) a first request to the network device to configure the UE with the second set of RF parameters; and if the anomalous traffic condition has occurred, transmitting (2070) a second request to the network device to configure the UE with a third set of RF parameters selected to alleviate the anomalous traffic condition. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 14, wherein the operations further comprise at least one operation performed by the UE of one of claims 2 to 11.
